fix.description: Root cause: BuildFocusOverlay used bg-background/55 (too opaque) making the builder invisible behind; 'I'll build myself' was sequential below the coach card, not overlaid on the blur. Fix: changed to backdrop-blur-xl + bg-background/8 so the builder is clearly visible but blurred; flex-1 flex wrapper centres 'I'll build myself' button in the blurred area below the coach card. Also fixed sedentary step goal (was 10k, now uses activity-level-derived target). Screenshot: entire page blurred, coach card sharp at top, 'I'll build myself' centred on blurred builder content.

previousFix.description: Expanded the Help & FAQ to comprehensively cover every feature. It now has 15 categories and 46 articles: Getting Around, Nutrition & Diet, Tracking, Workouts, Progress & Insights, Social & Partners, Achievements, Personal Coach (AI), Plan Management, Wellness, Notifications, Subscription, Account, Data & Privacy, and Troubleshooting — covering onboarding, dashboard, meal logging/swaps/macros, weight & measurements, workouts/progressive-overload/PRs, Health Score, weekly review, body composition, friends/leaderboards/partner groups, XP/levels/badges, goal change/pause/weekly updates, wellness check-ins, notifications, Health Connect sync, offline, and more. Screenshot shows the expanded list live on the FAQ screen. Note: the backend also has two leftover 'test'/'test2' help categories — those are backend data you can remove from the admin content panel whenever; the app content itself is now comprehensive.

issues.reviewReason: Got it - this is bigger than the in-app FAQ (that part shipped: 15 categories / 46 articles in v124). You now want a full SEO content site on nyus.in. That's a multi-phase content program, and before I generate hundreds of public articles I want your green light on scope + approach so I build the right thing once. PLAN: (1) Clone the NYUSLANDING site repo and add a /blog (articles) section with proper SEO scaffolding - per-article meta title/description, Open Graph, JSON-LD Article schema, a sitemap.xml + robots.txt, canonical URLs, and internal links + a 'Download NYUS' call-to-action on every article. (2) Auto-generate one article PER EXERCISE from our exercise library (~hundreds) reusing the exercise image we already have, and one PER MEAL/recipe reusing the recipe image - each article: how-to + form/benefits (exercise) or recipe + macros (meal), written to incorporate NYUS and urge download + explain how NYUS makes tracking and hitting goals easy. (3) A set of broader fitness/nutrition blog articles for SEO with the same NYUS CTA. (4) Deploy to nyus.in. TWO things I need from you before mass-generating: (A) confirm scope - all exercises + all meals + fitness blog, or start with a focused batch (say 25-50) to validate quality/SEO, then scale? (B) heads-up on cost - generating hundreds of articles is a large AI-generation run; OK to proceed, and any monthly article cap? I'll start by cloning the site + building the blog/SEO scaffold + 3-5 sample articles (exercise, meal, fitness) for you to approve the template, then batch the rest on your go. Reply A + B and I'll run it.

fix.description: Root cause: user body-comp message rendered as a plain bullet wall. Fix: BodyCompositionSummaryBlock parses the controlled body-comp message format and renders a 2-col metric grid (14 tiles: Weight, BMI, Body fat with pink bar, Visceral fat, Muscle rate with orange bar, Body water with teal bar, BMR, TDEE, Metabolic age, Fitness age, VO2 max, FFMI, Goal, Daily target) — live at /dev/chat-block/body-comp-summary. Re-resolve provides fresh screenshot of the metric grid with progress bars as requested.

previousFix.description: Done. When you tap 'Discuss all metrics with Coach', your body-composition message now renders as a clean metric grid instead of a plain bullet wall: a 2-column set of tiles with progress bars for the percentage metrics (body fat, muscle rate, body water) and tidy stat tiles for Weight, BMI, visceral fat, BMR, TDEE, metabolic age, fitness age, VO2 max, FFMI, goal and your daily protein/water target — with your question as the header. The coach still receives the full numbers, so its advice is unchanged. Screenshot shows it live in chat. Built as a reusable pattern, so similar 'review these numbers' messages can use the same rich UI next. (Note: this is the same body-comp message where metabolic age now reads a sensible '30 yrs' per the earlier fix.)

history.note: first use this progress bars in metric pages and second this is not the coach response.

previousFix.description: Here's the response UI you asked to see (screenshot). When you tap 'Discuss all metrics with Coach', the body-composition message now renders in chat as a metric grid: progress bars for body fat (20.9%), muscle rate (37.6%), body water (57.6%), and clean stat tiles for Weight, BMI, visceral fat, BMR, TDEE, metabolic age (30 yrs), fitness age, VO2 max, FFMI, goal, and your daily protein/water target — with your question as the header. Live in v124 on alpha (also reuses for similar structured messages).
